First ChatGPT Plus Request: Vital Connection Part 1 – Nightmare Story In-Context Training
DATA:
Avatar: dog owners
Niche: dog training
Primary Goal: train your dog to be more obedient
Details: My dog was so unruly someone threw a rock at it
When: last week of summer, 1983
Pattern Interrupt Image: a rock
FORMULA:
I remember it like it was yesterday: the last week of the summer of 1983
I was with my two best friends in the world, Jim and Bob
We were on the way to the beach for a night of bonfires and star gazing
The whole way there, I was feeling so frustrated with Rufus, my 120-pound bulldog.
Rufus had been acting up more and more latelyhe even started gnawing on my best friend’s cat!
The cat and Jim were not fans of my dog, to say the least.
On top of it all, I was also feeling overwhelmed with all the responsibilities I had been carrying
and I was starting to feel like I was losing control of my life.
And one of those responsibilities that I was just ignoring out of fear or just laziness was training Rufus.
Even though I knew I was ready for a change, I needed one more thing to really push me over the edge and start taking training seriously.
We all decided to take a walk on the beach.
And then my worst fear came true: my dog, who had been running off leash, suddenly ran up to a group of people and started barking aggressively.
I tried to call him off, but he wouldn’t listen.
The people were scared and started shouting and throwing rocks at Rufus.
I was embarrassed and horrified, and felt like the whole beach was staring at me.
I quickly put my dog back on his leash and quickly took him away.
And then I heard the comment that changed my life:
That beast needs to be put down!
One of them threatened to call the pound and have Rufus taken away.
I picked up one of the rocks they had thrown at Rufus, trying to protect themselves.
And I have it on my desk to this very day.
That’s why I mentioned my lucky rock at the very start of this video:
That silly rock became a reminder to treat dog training seriouslyfor me, for Rufus, and for everyone around us.
But that night I felt like I had hit rock bottom.
I had failed to live up to the expectations, and I had embarrassed and scared people in the process.
So right then and there I made a vow to myself:
I will spend the time training Rufustraining my dog deservesand I will do it or else!
From that moment on, I dedicated myself to learning how to become a better dog owner.
I read books and articles, attended seminars and workshops, and sought out advice from experienced dog owners.
I even got certified as a professional dog trainer.
Those series of events led you to me
And right now, I’m going to share what I learnedso keep watching.
>
DATA:
Avatar: women over 50
Niche: weight loss
Primary Goal: lose weight by kicking sugar addiction
Details: I had an intense workout with my friend Bobby and thought I had a heart attack
When:  January 1, 2020
Pattern Interrupt Image: a hospital gown
FORMULA:
Thinking back, it was Jan 1, 2020
I was with my workout partner, Bobby.
We were on the way to the gym for an intense workout session.
And when I say intense, I meanwell, I was barely able to break a sweat before breathing so hard I felt like I’d die.
I was in horrible shape.
On top of it all, I was feeling so lowstruggling with my sugar addiction and my lack of motivation.
I knew I was ready for a changebut I was in that spot where you know you need to do something different
but you just can’t flip the mental switch.
Can you relate?
It turns out I needed one more thing to really push me over the edge
to wake me upget me to take my weight loss journey seriouslyand possibly even save my life.
Bobby and I had decided to try a new weight loss program together.
Let’s kick it off strong with a good cardio workout, said Bobby.
It was like dragging me theredigging my claws into the groundpreferring to watch paint dry rather than hit the gym.
But I said, Surebut only if we can hit the ice cream shop after as a reward!
He agreed! (Neither of us had much willpower back then : )
We get to the gym and I started on the treadmill.
At first, everything was fine
But then it happened:
I felt a tightness in my chest.
My heart rate jumped up so much I could feel my pulse beating in my neck.
Oh my GODI think I’m having a heart attack!
I jumped off the moving treadmill and tore my ACLthe ligament that keeps your knee in place!
I was in agony!
Bobby rushed me to the hospitalnot knowing if I was dying or just in horrible pain because of my knee.
They rushed me back into the ERand then my worst nightmare:
Then my worst fears came true:
I not only had tore my ACLthe ligament that keeps your knee in place
I had a TIAa mini-strokeat the age of 37!
It wasn’t a heart attack, but TIAs often result in a far more serious stroke within a few years.
If you don’t get serious about your diet and health ma’am, you’re not going to see 40.
The nurse brought in one of those embarrassing hospital gowns.
She must have been new, because she put it on me backwards
and when I got up to go to the bathroom, being helped by the staff
I must have given the hospital a flash or two because all I heard was laughing.
Laughing at meeven though I was a total wreck!
I was embarrassed and ashamedand terrified.
Absolute panic.
I felt so alone and helplesstotally confused on what to do next.
And the pain was awful.
I was drowning in a sea of self-doubt and despair, and thought I was going to be stuck this way forever.
It was right therein that hospital bedwearing that goofy hospital gown that I mentioned at the first of the video
what I now call my lucky hospital gown
that I made the decision that brought you here today:
I’m drawing the line in the sand!
Not only will I lose the weight and get myself healthywhen I figure it out I’ll share it with the world.
My mission became bigger than myself:
To share a solution to what may feel like hopeless weight gain with women the world over.
I’m happy to say I lost 78 pounds
became healthy and fit
ended my relentless sugar cravings
and best of all, avoided a serious stroke.
And now I’m here to share how I did itso keep watching.
>
DATA:
Avatar=men over 50
Niche=back pain
Name = John Smith
Details=I went to a Rush concert with my friend Steve
When=Back in college
Pattern Interrupt Image: a concert ticket
FORMULA:
I remember it like it was yesterday
My buddy Steve and I were on our way to the Rush concert in Chicago.
We had been planning that night for months and were both so excited
but for me, the excitement was mixed with dread.
I was in so much pain from a nagging back injury that had been plaguing me for years.
I was in constant painand it was getting worse.
I was taking painkillers just to get through the day.
I felt like I was stuck in a never-ending cycle of pain and frustration.
I knew I had to do something to get out of it, but I was so overwhelmed with all the options that I didn’t know where to start.
I also had this nagging feeling that I was letting myself and my family down by not doing something about it.
So I kept putting it off, hoping it would go away.
But it only kept getting worse.
We got to the concert and I was in so much pain, it was hard to enjoy the show.
But then something happened that changed my life:
One of the songs, Limelight, caught my attention like never before.
The lyrics that really connected with me were:
Living on a lighted stage
Approaches the unreal
For those who think and feel
In touch with some reality
Beyond the gilded cage.
It was like a lightbulb went off in my head.
I realized I had been living my entire life in a gilded cage, stuck in my own misery, unable to break out.
But the lyrics gave me hope.
I started thinking about what it would be like to be in touch with reality
to be free of pain and live a life of joy and freedom, instead of one of nagging pain and frustration.
That’s when I knew I had to do something.
I had to act, and act fast.
So I made a vow right there in the middle of the concert:
I’m going to get out of this cage, I’m going to take control and take my life back!
And I started taking action right away.
I read books, sought out advice from experts, and experimented with various treatments until I found the perfect combination that worked for me.
And before I knew it, I was back to my old self.
I had my life back.
So that’s why I call this concert ticket my lucky ticket:
It represented the moment I decided to take control of my life and start taking action.
It was a reminder of my vow to get out of the gilded cage and take back my life.
And now I’m here to share what I learnedso keep watching.
>
